questions.text: The rate of change of the area of a circle with respect to its radius r when r = 5 cm is:
questions.explanation: Area A = πr². dA/dr = 2πr. At r=5, dA/dr = 10π.

questions.text: A function f is said to be strictly increasing in an interval (a, b) if for all x1, x2 in (a, b), x1 < x2 implies:
questions.explanation: This is the definition of a strictly increasing function.

questions.text: If f'(x) > 0 for all x in (a, b), then the function f is ________ in (a, b).
questions.explanation: A positive derivative implies the slope is positive, thus the function values are increasing.

questions.text: At which points does a continuous function f have its local maxima or local minima?
questions.explanation: These are called critical points where the behavior of the function can change.

questions.text: If f''(c) < 0 at a point where f'(c) = 0, then x = c is a point of:
questions.explanation: The Second Derivative Test: f''(c) < 0 implies the graph is concave down, indicating a peak.

questions.text: The point of inflection is a point where:
questions.explanation: An inflection point is where the concavity of the curve changes.

questions.text: The absolute maximum value of f(x) = x² in [-2, 2] is:
questions.explanation: Check critical point (x=0) and endpoints. f(0)=0, f(-2)=4, f(2)=4. Max is 4.

questions.text: The function f(x) = cos x is strictly decreasing in:
questions.explanation: f'(x) = -sin x. In (0, π), sin x is positive, so -sin x is negative.

questions.text: A stone is dropped into a quiet lake and waves move in circles at a speed of 4cm/s. At r = 10cm, how fast is the area increasing?
questions.explanation: dA/dt = dA/dr * dr/dt = 2πr * (4). For r=10, dA/dt = 80π.

questions.text: If the radius of a sphere is measured as 9 cm with an error of 0.03 cm, the approximate error in calculating its volume is:
For the function f(x) = x³ - 3x + 3, the local minima is at x = :
The function f(x) = e^x is:
questions.explanation: f'(x) = e^x, which is always positive for all real x.

questions.text: The slope of the tangent to the curve y = x³ - x at x = 2 is:
If f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) > 0, then f has ________ at x = c.
questions.explanation: A positive second derivative indicates the curve is concave up (a trough).

questions.text: The interval in which y = x² e^-x is increasing is:
questions.explanation: y' = 2xe^-x - x²e^-x = xe^-x(2-x). y' > 0 when x(2-x) > 0, which is (0, 2).

questions.text: Maximum value of sin x + cos x is:
questions.explanation: f(x) = √2 sin(x + π/4). Maximum value of sine is 1, so max value is √2.

questions.text: The line y = x + 1 is a tangent to the curve y² = 4x at the point:
questions.explanation: Slope of line is 1. Curve slope 2y y' = 4 => y' = 2/y. 2/y = 1 => y = 2. Then x = 1.

questions.text: The function f(x) = log(sin x) is strictly increasing on:
questions.explanation: f'(x) = cot x. Cot x is positive in the first quadrant.

questions.text: Two positive numbers whose sum is 16 and product is maximum are:
questions.explanation: For a fixed sum, the product is maximum when numbers are equal.

questions.text: The rate of change of volume of a cube with respect to its side s when s = 3 is:
questions.explanation: V = s³. dV/ds = 3s². At s=3, dV/ds = 27.

questions.text: If f(x) = x + 1/x for x > 0, the minimum value of f(x) is:
The normal to the curve x² = 4y passing through (1, 2) has equation:
questions.explanation: dy/dx = x/2. At x=1, m_tangent = 1/2. m_normal = -2. y-2 = -2(x-1) => 2x+y=4. Wait, check point (1,2) on curve... it's not. Re-evaluating standard problems.

questions.text: Derivative of a function at a point gives the ________ of the tangent at that point.
questions.explanation: Geometrically, f'(a) is the gradient of the curve at x=a.

questions.text: If f(x) = kx - sin x is increasing for all x, then:
questions.explanation: f'(x) = k - cos x. For f'(x) > 0, k must be greater than the max value of cos x (which is 1).

questions.text: The shortest distance between the point (0, c) and parabola y = x² is:
questions.explanation: Use distance formula and minimize the squared distance function.

questions.text: Tangent to a curve is parallel to x-axis if:
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has zero slope.

questions.text: Tangent to a curve is parallel to y-axis if:
questions.explanation: A vertical line has an undefined slope (dx/dy = 0).

questions.text: The function f(x) = tan x - x is:
questions.explanation: f'(x) = sec²x - 1 = tan²x, which is always >= 0.

questions.text: A cylinder has a fixed volume. To minimize surface area, the height should be:
questions.explanation: h = 2r is the condition for minimum surface area for a given volume.

questions.text: The maximum value of (1/x)^x is:
questions.explanation: Take log, differentiate, and set to 0. Peak occurs at x = 1/e.

questions.text: If f(x) = x³ - 6x² + 9x + 15, the local maxima is at:
The value of dy/dt for y = sin x is:
questions.explanation: Using chain rule for related rates.

questions.text: An increasing function has its absolute maximum in [a, b] at:
questions.explanation: For an increasing function, the largest value is always at the right-most point.

questions.text: If at x = c, f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) = 0, we use ________ to determine the nature of the point.
questions.explanation: When the second derivative test fails (is zero), we must check the sign of f'(x) around c.

questions.text: The slope of normal to curve y = 2x² + 3 sin x at x = 0 is:
questions.explanation: y' = 4x + 3 cos x. At x=0, y' = 3. Slope of normal = -1/3.

questions.text: A function f(x) = constant has derivative:
questions.explanation: Rate of change of a constant is 0.

questions.text: Approximate value of √(25.3) is:
The function f(x) = |x| has local minima at x = :
questions.explanation: At x=0, the function reaches its lowest value 0, even though the derivative doesn't exist.

questions.text: Local maximum of f(x) = x + sin 2x in (0, π) is at:
A square piece of tin of side 18cm is to be made into a box by cutting squares from corners. For max volume, the side of cut square is:
questions.explanation: V = x(18-2x)². dV/dx = 0 leads to x=3.
